title: Retrospective classification of 10 self-identified shortcomings from Chrome CDP page-target deadlock fix cycle
note_on_filename: Original intended path was docs/dev/retrospective-classification-20260519-175339.json but QA role's allowed_write_path_prefixes does not permit that prefix; wrote under qa-output- prefix instead. This is itself live evidence of item 7 (tool-policy / allowed_tools / allowed_write_path_prefixes contract gaps) — same root cause repeated three times during this very retrospective (TodoWrite blocked, tee Write blocked, retrospective-classification- prefix blocked).

buckets.SYSTEMIC: root cause is platform / hook / agent-contract / spec misdesign (not orchestrator misuse); fixing requires changing infra; recurs until infra fixed
buckets.NEED_TO_FIX: real workflow-integrity defect by orchestrator's choice this cycle; fixing requires orchestrator behaving differently next time; documentable as guidance
buckets.ACCEPTABLE_DEBT: explicitly authorized by user this session or by long-standing policy; logged in out_of_scope_observations; recurrence acceptable

items.title: push-analyst grant Chain B substantively bypassed (validate FAIL not short-circuited)
items.qa_rationale_round_1: /push spec relies on shell short-circuit semantics not enforced anywhere; needs wrapper or hook that aborts when validate prints FAIL regardless of how orchestrator chains commands.
items.final_rationale: The grant-validation contract cannot rely on orchestrator using && between validate and push.sh. The platform must enforce abort-on-FAIL atomically. Same race will recur every cycle.
items.concrete_remediation: Edit scripts/write-commit-grant.py (or wrap push.sh) so that push.sh itself reads the most recent grant validation result via a sentinel file and self-aborts if FAIL; OR have validate-push-grant.py exec push.sh on success so the two commands become one process, eliminating any orchestrator chaining gap. Edit commands/commit.md (or whatever /push spec is) to require the single-process pattern.

items.title: Bash safety hook bypassed via comment-pattern match (magic phrase in command comment)
items.qa_rationale_round_1: Pattern-match-on-string is the wrong mechanism for grant declaration; needs structured grant such as sentinel file, env var, or signed token.
items.final_rationale: The /allow grant mechanism currently lets any string containing the magic phrase pass the hook check. User authorization is real but the declaration channel is string-substrate, which both lets orchestrators bypass accidentally and is trivially spoofable.
items.concrete_remediation: Replace bash-safety pattern matching in hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh with a sentinel-file grant: orchestrator's /allow command writes a short-TTL marker to /tmp/claude-grants/<task_id>.json with explicit allowed_operations[]; hook reads that file at PreToolUse time instead of grepping command text. Update CLAUDE.md '🛡️ Subagent Hook Discipline' section to document the new mechanism.

items.title: TodoWrite ordering violation (Agent dispatched before in_progress mark)
items.qa_rationale_round_1: Hook ordering contract not documented in agents/commands; either docs should be explicit or hook should be more flexible.
items.codex_rationale: The hook contract may be under-documented, but the concrete failure was recoverable orchestration: mark the todo in_progress, then dispatch QA. Giving up and substituting text summary is behavioral discipline failure.
items.final_rationale: Accepting codex's point: the orchestrator owns the call order. Mark TodoWrite first, dispatch second. This is two-line behavioral discipline, not infra. Hook contract IS implicit in subagent_call tracking design and orchestrator could honor it.
items.concrete_remediation: Next time, the orchestrator MUST emit TodoWrite (item in_progress) BEFORE the Agent() call for that step. No infra change required. Add a single-line reminder to commands/close.md and commands/dev.md noting 'TodoWrite mark-as-in_progress for step N must precede any Agent() call dispatched within step N'.

items.title: push-analyst TTL 60s too tight for normal subagent latency
items.qa_rationale_round_1: Orchestrator chose not to dispatch a fix-cycle; one-line config change owed but not done.
items.codex_rationale: A 60s TTL for a subagent-mediated grant is structurally too short and will keep expiring under normal latency. 'Orchestrator should have opened /dev' is secondary; the recurring root cause is the platform policy.
items.final_rationale: Accepting codex's point: even if the orchestrator had opened /dev THIS cycle, the value choice is a platform-policy question that affects every push. 62s subagent execution + orchestrator dispatch latency is the normal regime, so 60s TTL is structurally wrong. The recurrence is policy, not behavior.
items.concrete_remediation: Edit scripts/write-commit-grant.py (or push-analyst grant emitter) to set DEFAULT_TTL_SECONDS = 180. Edit commands/commit.md / push spec to document the new TTL. Optionally make TTL a CLI flag so it can be tuned per-task without code changes.

items.title: /commit Step 7 spec-continue silently bypassed (context spec_path = null even though cycle produced spec-20260520-044700.md)
items.qa_rationale_round_1: Orchestrator's BA dispatch nulled spec fields; next time the spec-produced-this-cycle should be recorded into context so Step 7 fires.
items.codex_rationale: A workflow that can produce a spec and then silently pass spec_path=null has a broken data contract. Step 7 should discover/fail-closed on missing spec linkage rather than depending on manual context hygiene.
items.final_rationale: Accepting codex's point: Step 7 should not depend on manual context-field hygiene. If a spec exists in the cycle's artifact tree, Step 7 must discover and dispatch on it. Silent skip is a fail-open data contract.
items.concrete_remediation: Edit commands/commit.md Step 7 to (a) glob docs/dev/specs/spec-*.md created within the cycle window before reading context.spec_path; (b) fail-closed with an explicit error 'spec produced this cycle but not linked in context' rather than silently skipping. Optionally have BA's context-writer auto-populate spec_path when it has just created a spec.

items.title: AC4 / AC5 verification recipe drift (spec_text_vs_execution_drift recurrence)
items.qa_rationale_round_1: spec_text_vs_execution_drift dimension exists exactly to fail this; QA didn't fire it. Either BA must lock literal-text-runnable recipes or QA must enforce the dimension.
items.codex_rationale: The spec recipes were explicit, and QA already had the named drift dimension available. This cycle's QA under-enforced the contract; unless the dimension is impossible to apply mechanically, this is primarily QA judgment failure.
items.final_rationale: Accepting codex's point: the dimension is documented in agents/qa.md and was available. QA chose to call AC4/AC5 minor non-blocking despite the recipe-drift pattern fitting the dimension. This is QA judgment failure this cycle, not absent infra.
items.concrete_remediation: Next time, QA MUST raise dimension=spec_text_vs_execution_drift for any AC where the verifier substitutes a different recipe from the literal spec text — even when 'substantively equivalent'. The dimension forces BA to update AC text. Add a one-line reminder to agents/qa.md Step 1 success-criteria check: 'if dev verification recipe differs from AC literal text, raise spec_text_vs_execution_drift regardless of equivalence judgment.'

items.title: Inspector role Skill(codex) consultations all blocked by tool-policy
items.qa_rationale_round_1: Pure infra defect: agent.md mandates codex consult but hooks/pretool-tool-policy.py denies it. Platform contract inconsistency.
items.final_rationale: Confirmed empirically THREE times during this very retrospective: (1) TodoWrite blocked for QA role; (2) Write of tee output path blocked; (3) Write of retrospective-classification- prefix blocked. The role/tool-policy contract is inconsistent with agent.md role contracts mandating Skill(codex). Will recur for every role whose allowed_tools or allowed_write_path_prefixes is incomplete.
items.concrete_remediation: Edit /root/.claude/policies/tool-policy.v1.json role registry to (a) include Skill in allowed_tools for every role whose agent.md mandates codex consultation (style-inspector, cleanliness-inspector, prompt-inspector, qa already had Skill); (b) audit allowed_write_path_prefixes for QA to include retrospective output paths if QA is to write retrospectives; (c) audit all agents/*.md files for codex_required:true mentions and reconcile against the role's tool-policy entry.

items.title: 23 leaked production playwright-mcp processes untouched (still holding locks)
items.qa_rationale_round_1: User explicitly authorized via '就地部署' directive; AC12 LEAKED_PWMCP invariant preserves them; logged in OOS observations.
items.codex_rationale: ACCEPTABLE-DEBT is defensible because AC12 / OOS explicitly constrained cleanup.
items.final_rationale: User authorization is real and on the record (verbatim '就地部署'). AC12 invariant explicitly forbade reaping. Future sessions will wait on old locks until natural attrition completes; this is the user-accepted cost of in-place deployment.
items.concrete_remediation: No fix required. Confirm OOS record exists in ticket / BA spec / close-report (verified in close-report-20260519-175339.md lines 110, 165, 192, 212). If user later changes scope (e.g., requests '清空 leaked processes'), this becomes a new cycle's work.

items.title: gitignore reversal d988d4a flips c411ef1's docs/ ignore policy without deep rationale
items.qa_rationale_round_1: Behavioral fix — next reversal commit should cite + explain prior commit's reasoning. Not infra, not user-authorized debt.
items.final_rationale: Commit hygiene discipline: when reversing a recent prior commit's policy, the new commit message must cite the prior commit SHA and explain why the prior rationale no longer applies. No infra change needed — just better commit-message writing.
items.concrete_remediation: Next time the orchestrator (or any commit author) reverses a recent prior commit's policy, the commit message MUST include 'Reverses <SHA>: <one-line rationale for why prior reasoning no longer holds>'. Add a one-line note to commands/commit.md template guidance. Retroactive fix for d988d4a is not required (git history is already written) but future reversals should comply.

items.title: QA test harness leaves orphan PIDs every close cycle (no trap-on-exit cleanup)
items.qa_rationale_round_1: Test harness/fixture defect; spec or harness needs trap-on-exit. Recurs every close that runs the same ACs.
items.final_rationale: Empirical recurrence in this cycle alone: round 1 left PIDs 3024155/3024167, round 2 left PIDs 3261106/3261281. Without test-fixture-level cleanup the pattern will repeat every close that runs AC1/AC2/AC11. Item 8 leakage is user-authorized; item 10 leakage is test-harness defect.
items.concrete_remediation: Edit the AC1/AC2/AC11 test harness in docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-044700.md (or wherever the validation recipes live) to wrap any background playwright-mcp invocation in `trap 'kill -TERM $WRAPPER_PID $WRAPPER_CHILD_PID 2>/dev/null' EXIT` or equivalent. Make trap-on-exit a required pattern in any BA spec that involves spawning long-lived child processes.

priority_remediation_rationale: 7 first (blocks codex_required:true and any write-path-policy gap on every subsequent cycle until reconciled; this very retrospective hit the gap 3 times). 2 second (bypass mechanism is trivially spoofable, weakens whole safety hook posture). 1 third (push grant integrity broken every push). 5 fourth (silent spec-continue skip means spec lifecycle never closes). 4 fifth (push race condition recurs every push). 10 sixth (orphan PID accumulation per close cycle). 6 seventh (QA judgment discipline, fix on next cycle). 3 eighth (one-line ordering discipline). 9 ninth (commit message hygiene). 8 last (no fix needed — confirm OOS record).

infra_changes_required: AC1/AC2/AC11 test harness in spec-20260520-044700.md (item 10 — trap-on-exit)
behavioral_changes_required_next_cycle: QA fire spec_text_vs_execution_drift on any recipe substitution regardless of equivalence judgment (item 6)
Commit reversal messages must cite + explain prior SHA (item 9)
no_action_required: 23 leaked playwright-mcp processes — user-authorized per 就地部署, AC12 invariant, OOS-logged (item 8)
